commit.message:
fix: restore database.tmdl with compatibilityMode and use initializeConnection for first sync

- Restore database.tmdl with compatibilityMode: powerBI (required by Fabric Git integration parser)
- Workflow: when workspaceHead is null, call initializeConnection instead of updateFromGit
  The two endpoints serve different lifecycle states:
  - initializeConnection: first-time sync, no prior workspaceHead
  - updateFromGit: subsequent syncs with a known workspaceHead

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(build): retry SHA256 fetch until hash stabilises

GitHub CDN may serve a transitional re-compressed zip on first request.
Retry up to 5 times with 3s sleep, stopping once two consecutive fetches
produce the same hash.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(crypto): add HashSHA256 for hex-encoded SHA-256 hashing (#283)

commit.message:
Add portfolio-aware actioning and endangered symbols (#6)

This pull request adds support for passing portfolio positions and
watchlist entries to the Faultline CLI and core workflow, enabling
scenario runs to take into account user-specified holdings and
watchlists. It introduces new data models (`PortfolioPosition` and
`WatchlistEntry`), updates the CLI to accept these as inputs (via
command-line arguments or JSON files), and threads them through the
runner and action generation logic. The action engine now generates
targeted recommendations and risk assessments based on these inputs.

**CLI and Input Handling Enhancements:**
- Added new CLI arguments (`--positions`, `--watchlist`,
`--positions-json`, `--watchlist-json`) to the `run-demo`, `run-live`,
and `run-latest` commands, allowing users to specify portfolio positions
and watchlists either as comma-separated lists or JSON files. Helper
functions `_parse_symbol_list` and `_load_structured_list` were
introduced to parse these inputs.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-86e5bb6f881d90a709282f43bcefb3561da9c8f5a8c3728fcb609832908ad195R18-R42)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-86e5bb6f881d90a709282f43bcefb3561da9c8f5a8c3728fcb609832908ad195R59-R71)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-86e5bb6f881d90a709282f43bcefb3561da9c8f5a8c3728fcb609832908ad195L86-R116)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-86e5bb6f881d90a709282f43bcefb3561da9c8f5a8c3728fcb609832908ad195L119-R167)

**Data Model Additions:**
- Introduced `PortfolioPosition` and `WatchlistEntry` Pydantic models in
`contracts.py` and updated imports/exports in `models/__init__.py` and
`models/state.py` to support them throughout the codebase.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-82d39660fbdd6c8bba269ee163dc1f729748c3bc17e3774cd5ec96103f691056R265-R280)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ab2bc88bac0f5d159c2acf98f95d2e9c8823a37a3ae3c35f6d6ada70ca3796f3R21)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-ab2bc88bac0f5d159c2acf98f95d2e9c8823a37a3ae3c35f6d6ada70ca3796f3R37)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-ab2bc88bac0f5d159c2acf98f95d2e9c8823a37a3ae3c35f6d6ada70ca3796f3R61)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-ab2bc88bac0f5d159c2acf98f95d2e9c8823a37a3ae3c35f6d6ada70ca3796f3R77)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-90f2941538ae9e75d281fc71ee2ea009430d1a668a083c74ee8695dd9166a448R14-R21)

**Core Workflow and Runner Updates:**
- Updated `StrategicSwarmRunner` and its `_run` method to accept and
validate lists of `PortfolioPosition` and `WatchlistEntry` objects,
ensuring these are included in the workflow state for all run modes.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5b94f49dea89e2b339ea71e3426575ab69038e132e488bf6b288a12a3cb20972L10-R17)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5b94f49dea89e2b339ea71e3426575ab69038e132e488bf6b288a12a3cb20972L42-R98)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-5b94f49dea89e2b339ea71e3426575ab69038e132e488bf6b288a12a3cb20972R226-R233)

**Action Generation Logic Improvements:**
- Enhanced the action engine (`system_first.py`) to generate
personalized action recommendations and exit signals based on the
provided portfolio positions and watchlist. Added methods to assess
conviction and determine endangered symbols, which are now included in
the workflow state and final report.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-30b52361e30f9fc37d336cc8bb773cdb40c61d85115372ff0ad2ec17263ec001L487-R497)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-30b52361e30f9fc37d336cc8bb773cdb40c61d85115372ff0ad2ec17263ec001L540-R554)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-30b52361e30f9fc37d336cc8bb773cdb40c61d85115372ff0ad2ec17263ec001R567-R671)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-6a99700c72459cf53e1b272a5c77b032c6e1eec8e3503b776170c61cbb43bb03R246-R260)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-6a99700c72459cf53e1b272a5c77b032c6e1eec8e3503b776170c61cbb43bb03R282)

**Final Report Extension:**
- Extended the `FinalReport` model to include an `endangered_symbols`
field, capturing symbols in the portfolio at risk due to high-conviction
negative implications.

These changes make the system more responsive to user-specific contexts,
enabling more actionable and relevant output.

---------

Co-authored-by: Matteo Longo <matteo.longo@ip-10-102-128-209.eu-west-1.compute.internal>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: matteolongo <3891387+matteolongo@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(sanitize): add Truncate for rune-safe string truncation (#277)

Truncate shortens strings to at most maxLen runes, appending an
ellipsis character when truncated. Safe for Unicode text.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(security): add RedactURL to strip credentials from URLs (#276)

RedactURL removes userinfo (username:password) from URL strings to
prevent credential leakage in logs and error messages.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(auth): add Music Assistant as web UI auth provider

Add MA credentials as a third authentication backend alongside HA
login_flow and local password. When AUTH_ENABLED is True and the bridge
is connected to MA, users can sign in with their MA username/password.

Backend:
- Add _detect_auth_methods() to auto-detect available methods (ma/ha/password)
- Add _ma_validate_credentials() using existing _ma_http_login() from api_ma
- Route login POST by method field instead of ha_mode boolean
- Pass auth_methods list to login template for dynamic UI

Frontend (login.html):
- Tab-based method switcher when multiple methods available
- Separate forms for MA, HA, and local password
- Empty state message when no auth method is configured

Config UI:
- Auth hint shows available sign-in methods when enabled
- Password labeled as optional when MA is connected

Tests:
- 10 new tests for method detection and credential validation
- All 148 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Useful for ZIP codes, PINs, and numeric reference codes stored as strings.

feat(request): add ClientIP for extracting client IP address (#274)

ClientIP checks X-Forwarded-For (first entry), then X-Real-IP, then
falls back to RemoteAddr with the port stripped.

Addr reads and validates a host:port address from an environment
variable using net.SplitHostPort. Validates port range 1-65535.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ruff format compliance in src/faultline/__main__.py (#19)

PR #6 introduced three pairs of inline ternary expressions for
`positions`/`watchlist` parsing that exceeded the 120-char line-length
limit, causing `ruff format --check` to fail in CI.

Applied `ruff format` to break each oversized ternary into a
parenthesized multi-line form:

```python
# Before (>120 chars, CI failure)
positions = _load_structured_list(args.positions_json) if args.positions_json is not None else _parse_symbol_list(args.positions)

# After
positions = (
    _load_structured_list(args.positions_json)
    if args.positions_json is not None
    else _parse_symbol_list(args.positions)
)
```

Affected blocks: `run-demo`, `run-live`, and `run-latest` command
handlers.

## Type of Change

- [ ] New feature (provider, node, archetype, capability)
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)
- [ ] Documentation
- [x] CI / tooling

## Checklist

- [x] `ruff check src/ tests/` reports no errors
- [x] `ruff format --check src/ tests/` reports no changes needed
- [x] New behavior has test coverage (or existing tests cover it)
- [x] No `TODO` / `FIXME` markers left in changed files
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` updated if architecture or key patterns changed

## Testing Done

```
$ ruff format src/faultline/__main__.py
1 file reformatted

$ ruff format --check src/faultline/__main__.py
1 file already formatted

$ ruff check src/faultline/__main__.py
All checks passed!
```

## Screenshots / Output (if relevant)

N/A — formatting-only change, no runtime behavior affected.

Cfodev 1538 provider feedback dashboard for service desk (#936)

* Dashboard - Provider feedback

Renames QA under Dashboard menu to Provider feedback
Removes QA - Team under Dashboard menu

* Rename DashboardQA to DashboardProviderFeedback and remove DashboardQATeam

Renamed DashboardQA component to DashboardProviderFeedback
Updated class name to match new component name
Removed unused DashboardQATeam component and its code-behind file

* Configure Provider Feedback dashboard access and navigation

 Updates authorisation policy from Internal to Qa2 for Provider Feedback dashboard
 Adds Provider Feedback to search dialog for QA2 roles
Remove QAOfficer from allowed roles, restrict to QA2 (QASupportManager, QAManager, SMT, SystemSupport)

* Enhance Provider Feedback dashboard with activity type breakdown and data completeness

     - Adds separate charts for Education/Training, Employment, and ISW Support activities
     - Fills missing queue/contract combinations with zero counts for consistent visualisation
     - Removes sortable from Message column headers

* Updates headings

Updates page and tab labels

* Migrations:Activities

Adds FeedbackType field to Activities.EscalationQueueNote and Activities.Qa2QueueNote tables

* Activities: Add FeedbackType field to QA2 and Escalation queue responses

- Add FeedbackType enum (Advisory, AcceptedByException) to Domain
- Update QA2 and Escalation response commands to accept FeedbackType
- Add FeedbackType radio buttons in QA2 and Escalation UI (shown only when Accept response is selected)
- Clear FeedbackType when response changes from Accept to other options

* Migrations:Enrolments

Adds FeedbackType field to Enrolment.EscalationQueueNote and Enrolment.Qa2QueueNote tables

* Enrolments: Add FeedbackType field to QA2 and Escalation queue responses

- Add FeedbackType radio buttons in Enrolment QA2 and Escalation UI (shown only when Accept response is selected)
- Clear FeedbackType when response changes from Accept to other options
- Update QA2 and Escalation response commands to accept FeedbackType

* Add Activities Advisories dashboard tab to Provider Feedback

- Create GetActivitiesAdvisoriesToProvider query with tabular and chart data DTOs
- Implement query handler to fetch activities with Advisory feedback type from QA2 and Escalation queues
- Add ActivitiesAdvisoriesToProviderDashboardComponent with visual and tabular modes
- Display separate charts for Education/Training, Employment, and ISW Support activities
- Show advisory data grouped by contract and queue (QA2/Escalation) with stacked bar charts
- Include tabular view with participant, support worker, PQA user, CFO user, dates, and advisory notes
- Integrate component into Provider Feedback dashboard "Activities: Advisories" tab

* Add Enrolments Advisories dashboard tab to Provider Feedback

- Create GetEnrolmentAdvisoriesToProvider query with tabular and chart data DTOs
- Implement query handler to fetch enrolments with Advisory feedback type for QA2 and Escalation queues
- Add EnrolmentAdvisoriesToProviderDashboardComponent with visual and tabular modes
- Show advisory data grouped by contract and queue (QA2/Escalation) with stacked bar charts
- Include tabular view with participant, support worker, PQA user, CFO user, dates, and advisory notes etc.
- Integrate component into Provider Feedback dashboard "Enrolments: Advisories" tab

* Rename SubmittedDate to PqaSubmittedDate for clarity in provider dashboards

Updated property and column header to clarify submission is by Provider QA.

* Migrations - Add ProviderFeedbackEnrolment and  ProviderFeedbackEnrolment entities

* Populates FeedbackType field in Activities Note and Enrolment Notes tables

Populates FeedbackType field in following tables:
-[Enrolment].[Qa2QueueNote]
-[Enrolment].[EscalationNote]
-[Activities].[Qa2QueueNote]
-[Activities].[EscalationNote]

* Change ProviderFeedbackEnrolment and ProviderFeedbackActivities

-Remove  NoteCreatedDate  and NoteCreatedBy fields from both entities
-Add ActivityId and ActivityType to the ProviderFeedback entity

* Add migration to populate ProviderFeedbackActivity and ProviderFeedbackEnrolment from:

- Enrolment QA2 and Escalation queues (returned and advisory feedback)
- Activity QA2 and Escalation queues (returned and advisory feedback)

* Update Queries

Updates Enrolment Advisory and Returns queries to look at ProviderFeedbackEnrolments
Updates Activity Advisory and Returns queries to look at ProviderFeedbackActivities

* Add Enrolment Feedback consumers and Activity feedback Integration event and consumers

     - Create RecordActivityReturnedFeedbackConsumer to track activity returns
     - Create RecordEnrolmentReturnedFeedbackConsumer to track enrolment returns
       - Triggers on SubmittedToAuthority → SubmittedToProvider transition
       - Records latest QA2/Escalation feedback in ProviderFeedbackActivity table

     - Create RecordActivityAdvisoryFeedbackConsumer to track activity approvals with advisory feedback
     - Create RecordEnrolmentAdvisoryFeedbackConsumer to track enrolment approvals with advisory feedback
       - Triggers on SubmittedToAuthority → Approved transition
       - Only records feedback when FeedbackType is 0 (Returned) or 1 (Advisory)
       - Add logging to all feedback consumers

* Update src/Application/Features/Dashboard/Queries/GetActivitiesAdvisoriesToProvider.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* removed unused properties

* fix: Correct migration order to create tables before backfilling data

* fix: Correct migration order - add ActivityId columns before backfill

* fix: Include full end date in provider feedback dashboard queries

-Change date filter from <= EndDate to < EndDate.AddDays(1) to include records created throughout the entire end date, not just at midnight.

* Added N/A option

Added N/A option to QA2 and Escalation response screens, this is to counter the inability to unselect radio buttons

* Refactor: save internal and external QA messages as separate notes

-Remove IsMessageExternal flag and use chained AddNote calls to save
-Message (internal) and MessageToProvider (external) as separate notes within the same transaction. Add FeedbackType enum values.

* fix backfill migration scripts

filter out QA2 escalations from Enrolments and Activities returns list
filter out QA2 escalation when populating Mi tables for Enrolments and Activities

* Make internal message mandatory

When escalating, Internal Message is required  for both Enrolments and Activities
When comment/defer, Internal Message is required  for both Enrolments and Activities

* Updated migration script to backfill returns

Updated script to backfill FeedbackType.Returns in QA2 and Escalation Queue Notes  for Enrolments
Updated script to backfill FeedbackType.Returns in QA2 and Escalation Queue Notes  for Activities

* resolves merge conflicts and addresses copilot code review suggestions.

- Moves business logic from Razor views to code-behind files for Escalation and QA2 pages
- Removes unnecessary validation rule for Comment responses in escalation workflow
- Adds clarifying comments to escalation response validation rules
- Reorders dashboard tabs to group advisory feedback sections together
- Standardises property usage to MessageToProvider across QA workflows

NoWhitespace rejects values containing spaces, tabs, newlines, or
carriage returns. Useful for usernames, API keys, and other
identifiers that must be a single token.

commit.message:
feat(security): add MaskEmail for safe email logging (#263)

MaskEmail keeps the first character and domain of an email address,
replacing the rest of the local part with asterisks. Useful for
audit logs and error messages.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(config): add Int64 and MustInt64 for large integer env vars (#262)

Int64 reads a 64-bit integer with a default fallback. MustInt64
panics if the variable is unset or not a valid int64. Useful for
database IDs, timestamps, and file sizes.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(sanitize): add Slugify for URL-friendly slug generation (#261)

Slugify lowercases the string, replaces non-alphanumeric characters
with hyphens, collapses consecutive hyphens, and trims edge hyphens.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(request): add ContentType and IsJSON helpers (#260)

ContentType extracts the media type from the Content-Type header,
stripping parameters. IsJSON is a convenience check for
application/json.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(middleware): add NoCache for cache-prevention headers (#259)

NoCache sets Cache-Control, Pragma, and Expires headers to prevent
browsers and proxies from caching API responses.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

UX: polish tenant "Prijavi kvar" form + audit category/priority whitelist alignment (#469)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct tenant issue payload to match SU_sanitizeIssue storage contract

- Rename buildingId -> bid (critical: was causing SU_sanitizeIssue to return null)
- Rename createdBy -> createdByUser, add createdByRole: ROLE_STANAR
- Change priority default/fallback from "normal" to "medium" (not in valid list)
- Fix HTML select options: replace invalid "normal" option with low/medium/high/urgent

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: show tenant-submitted issue marker in issues list and detail

- List row: adds "Prijavio stanar [· username]" in .issMeta when createdByRole === "stanar"
- Detail panel: adds "Prijavio: [username|stanar]" meta item with --stanar highlight
- styles.css: two minimal rules (.issMetaRole--stanar, .issDetailMetaItem--stanar) for blue highlight
- Graceful: badge omitted when createdByRole is missing; username omitted when createdByUser is missing

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: align kvar form categories with SU_sanitizeIssue whitelist

The prijaviKvarCat <select> had 'instalacije' and 'elektrika' options which
are not in SU_sanitizeIssue's VALID_CATEGORIES. Selecting them caused the
category to be silently remapped to "ostalo" on every save/reload.

Replaced form options with the full sanitizer whitelist:
ostalo (default/selected), lift, struja, voda, interfon, vrata, ciscenje

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* ux: polish Prijavi kvar form labels, placeholders and option text

- Title label: "Naslov" → "Kratki naslov"
- Desc label: "Opis" → "Opis kvara"
- Desc placeholder: guides tenant to describe location, symptom and duration
- Category options: raw keys → readable Serbian (values unchanged)
- Priority options: raw English → Serbian labels (values unchanged)
- Validation errors updated to match new labels

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ui): reload config form after MA login to preserve new token

After successful MA login, the backend saves the new token directly to
config.json. But the config form still had the OLD token in its hidden
field, so 'Save and Restart' would overwrite the new token with the old.

Now calls loadConfig() after login to refresh the form with the
backend-saved values before marking dirty.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(middleware): add SecureHeaders for common security response headers (#245)

SecureHeaders sets X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options,
and Referrer-Policy headers on every response, protecting
against clickjacking and MIME-type sniffing.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(crypto): add GenerateUUID for crypto-secure UUID v4 (#244)

GenerateUUID creates RFC 4122 v4 UUIDs using crypto/rand.
No external dependency needed — pure stdlib implementation.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): always try direct HTTP fallback on library login failure

The music_assistant_client library raises 'Invalid username or password'
for any 401 response, including format mismatches with MA beta. The
previous code short-circuited the direct HTTP fallback when it saw
'invalid' in the error message, preventing login from ever succeeding
with MA beta 2.8+.

Now always falls through to _ma_http_login() which correctly tries
both body formats and reports the actual error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
docs(request): add examples for ParseCommaSeparatedInts, ParseDateParam, RequireUUIDParam (#243)

Three new example tests for remaining request helpers without
runnable documentation.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(middleware): add MaxBody request size limiter (#242)

MaxBody returns middleware that limits incoming request body
size using http.MaxBytesReader, protecting against oversized
payload attacks. Handlers see a 413 when the limit is exceeded.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(security): add IsRelativeURL to prevent open redirects (#241)

IsRelativeURL validates that a URL is a relative path (starts
with / but not //) with no host or scheme, preventing open
redirect vulnerabilities when using user-supplied redirect targets.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): support MA beta 2.8+ login format

MA beta 2.8.0b19 changed /auth/login to expect credentials in a
nested dict: {"credentials": {"username": ..., "password": ...}}
instead of flat {"username": ..., "password": ...}.

Add _ma_http_login() fallback that tries both formats when the
music_assistant_client library fails. Also update _get_ma_oauth_params()
to try both stable GET and beta JSONRPC endpoints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
feat(cache): add Keys method for inspecting cache contents (#240)

Keys returns a snapshot of all keys from most recently used to
least recently used. Useful for debugging and monitoring.

feat(scan): add Value[T] for single-column scalar queries (#238)

Value scans a single scalar value from a SingleRowScanner,
useful for SELECT count(*), SELECT max(id), etc. Avoids the
need for a scanFn when only one column is returned.

access control.

feat(response): add Redirect helper (#236)

Thin wrapper around http.Redirect for consistency with the
response package API. Supports any HTTP redirect status code.

Redesign website based on feedback

- Update color scheme (#4D0921, #EBE2CD, #80455F, #3C3333)
- Change fonts to League Gothic + Special Elite
- Restructure nav to 5 dropdown menus (Home, Join Us, Logistics, Support, Contact)
- Add countdown timer to hero section (May 16, 2026)
- Replace SVG placeholders with optimized real photos
- Restructure Join Us: Eventbrite, Marshall, Collaborate, WhatsApp
- Remove What to Expect section and Facilities card
- Add Schedule card, parking permit link
- Move Sign Up for Updates to Contact section
- Add social media links (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, WhatsApp)
- Add YouTube video promo link to About section
- Fix IBAN (3265 -> 3365), update PayPal donate link
- Redesign footer with social icons and simplified links
- Add all external links (Eventbrite, forms, Dropbox, socials)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(request): add examples for 7 query-parsing helpers (#232)

New examples for OptionalQueryParam, OptionalQueryInt,
RequireQueryParamInt, ParseCommaSeparated, ParseSortOrder,
ParseEnumParam, and ParseBoolParam companion examples.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(config): add examples for StringRequired, StringSlice, StringMap, Validate (#231)

Four new example tests demonstrating:
- StringRequired: required env var with error on missing
- StringSlice: comma-separated list parsing
- StringMap: key=value pair parsing
- Validate: batch required-env-var checking

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(testkit): add AssertEmpty and AssertNotEmpty helpers (#230)

AssertEmpty fails when the string is non-empty, AssertNotEmpty
fails when empty. These provide clearer intent than
AssertEqual(t, s, "") and AssertNotEqual(t, s, "").

51 adoption candidates across the codebase.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(security): add SanitizeHeader to prevent CRLF injection (#229)

SanitizeHeader strips \r and \n characters from a string,
preventing HTTP header injection attacks. Includes 6 tests
and a benchmark.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
